2026-04-25T18:18:51.8319525Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-25T18:18:51.8320042Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-25T18:18:51.8320433Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-25T18:18:51.8320784Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-25T18:18:51.8321067Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-25T18:18:51.8321307Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-25T18:18:51.8321465Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-25T18:18:51.8321630Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-25T18:18:51.8321805Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-25T18:18:51.8322021Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-25T18:18:51.8322204Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-25T18:18:51.8322482Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-25T18:18:51.8322721Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-25T18:19:57.4648721Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-25T18:19:44.196713273Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58681,\"title\":\"[mgmt-generator] AddTag/SetTags/RemoveTag emit \\new TPatch() for TrackedResource patches, leaving Tags=null (NRE in the patch fallback path)\",\"body\":\"### Summary\\n\\nThe mgmt code generator emits broken `AddTag` / `SetTags` / `RemoveTag` methods on `ArmResource` types whose backing patch model derives from `TrackedResourceData`. The fallback (non-`TagResource`) branch instantiates the patch via the **internal parameterless constructor** instead of the public `[InitializationConstructor]`, leaving `Tags = null` and causing a `NullReferenceException` at runtime.\\n\\nThis affects **every TrackedResource generated from TypeSpec** when the user-supplied subscription does not have the generic Tags resource available (i.e. `CanUseTagResource(...)` returns `false`).\\n\\n### Repro\\n\\nRepository: `Azure.ResourceManager.ManagedServiceIdentities` (migrated from autorest.csharp to TypeSpec mgmt generator). Run the existing recorded tests in playback:\\n\\n```\\ncd sdk/managedserviceidentity/Azure.ResourceManager.ManagedServiceIdentities/tests\\n$env:AZURE_TEST_MODE = \\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;Playback\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;\\ndotnet test -f net10.0 --filter \\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;FullyQualifiedName~AddTag|FullyQualifiedName~SetTags|FullyQualifiedName~RemoveTag\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;\\n```\\n\\nResult:\\n```\\nTotal tests: 18\\n     Passed: 12   ← (True) and (null) cases — go through the TagResource branch\\n     Failed:  6   ← (False) cases — go through the patch branch\\n```\\n\\nStack trace for `SetTags(False)`:\\n```\\nSystem.NullReferenceException : Object reference not set to an instance of an object.\\n   at Azure.ResourceManager.SharedExtensions.ReplaceWith(IDictionary`2 dest, IDictionary`2 src)\\n        sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/src/Shared/SharedExtensions.cs:47\\n   at Azure.ResourceManager.ManagedServiceIdentities.UserAssignedIdentityResource.SetTags(IDictionary`2 tags, ...)\\n        sdk/managedserviceidentity/.../src/Generated/UserAssignedIdentityResource.cs:568\\n```\\n\\n### Root cause\\n\\nThe emitted else-branch (in `Generated/UserAssignedIdentityResource.cs`):\\n\\n```csharp\\nelse\\n{\\n    UserAssignedIdentityData current = (await GetAsync(...)).Value.Data;\\n    UserAssignedIdentityPatch patch = new UserAssignedIdentityPatch();   // ← BUG\\n    foreach (KeyValuePair tag in current.Tags)\\n        patch.Tags.Add(tag);                                              // NRE: patch.Tags == null\\n    patch.Tags[key] = value;\\n    Response result = await UpdateAsync(patch, ...);\\n    return Response.FromValue(result.Value, result.GetRawResponse());\\n}\\n```\\n\\n`UserAssignedIdentityPatch` exposes three constructors:\\n\\n| Ctor | Visibility | Purpose | Tags state |\\n|------|------------|---------|------------|\\n| `UserAssignedIdentityPatch(AzureLocation)` | `public` | `[InitializationConstructor]` (chained to `TrackedResourceData(AzureLocation)`) | `Tags = new ChangeTrackingDictionary()` ✅ |\\n| `UserAssignedIdentityPatch(...)` (full args) | `internal` | `[SerializationConstructor]` | uses caller-supplied `tags` |\\n| `UserAssignedIdentityPatch()` | `internal` | parameterless deserialization ctor (chains to `TrackedResourceData()`) | `Tags = null` ❌ |\\n\\n`TrackedResourceData` (in `Azure.ResourceManager`):\\n\\n```csharp\\n[InitializationConstructor]\\nprotected TrackedResourceData(AzureLocation location)\\n{\\n    Tags = new ChangeTrackingDictionary();\\n    Location = location;\\n}\\n\\ninternal protected TrackedResourceData()\\n{\\n    // intentionally empty — for deserialization only\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe generator already understands `[InitializationConstructor]` vs the deserialization ctor (it emits both). But its tag-method template ignores that distinction and always picks the parameterless ctor — which is `internal` and intended only for the deserialization framework to call before populating properties.\\n\\n### Fix\\n\\nIn the emitter template that produces `AddTag` / `SetTags` / `RemoveTag` (sync + async × 3), when the patch type derives from `TrackedResourceData`, emit:\\n\\n```diff\\n- UserAssignedIdentityPatch patch = new UserAssignedIdentityPatch();\\n+ UserAssignedIdentityPatch patch = new UserAssignedIdentityPatch(current.Location);\\n```\\n\\nThat is, instantiate the patch via its `[InitializationConstructor]` and pass `current.Data.Location` (already fetched on the line above). For non-tracked patches the existing `new TPatch()` remains correct.\\n\\n### Affected scope\\n\\nEvery mgmt SDK migrated to the TypeSpec mgmt generator whose resource is a `TrackedResource` and whose service does not expose a tags-only PATCH operation (so the generator falls back to building the patch object client-side). It compiles and ApiCompat passes, so the bug is invisible unless the package has tag-method tests that force the `else` branch.\\n\\n### Workaround in the meantime\\n\\nIn `src/Custom/{Resource}.cs`, `[CodeGenSuppress]` the 6 tag methods and re-implement them with `new TPatch(current.Location)`. See `sdk/managedserviceidentity/Azure.ResourceManager.ManagedServiceIdentities/src/Custom/UserAssignedIdentityResource.cs` for an example.\\n\\n### Environment\\n- Generator: `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt@1.0.0-alpha.20260421.1`\\n- TypeSpec compiler resolved at: `sdk/managedserviceidentity/.../TempTypeSpecFiles/node_modules/@typespec/compiler`\\n- Repro repo: `Azure/azure-sdk-for-net`, branch migrating `Azure.ResourceManager.ManagedServiceIdentities` from autorest.csharp to TypeSpec\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58681\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"haiyuazhang\",\"id\":1924967,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/haiyuazhang\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1924967?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\",\"CodeGen\"],\"assignees\":[\"haiyuazhang\",\"Copilot\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":2,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":2},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-25T18:17:33Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-25T18:19:13Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-04-25T18:19:57.4668756Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-25T18:19:44.206984102Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":58681,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-25T18:19:57.4691768Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-25T18:19:44.430256957Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58681,\"title\":\"[mgmt-generator] AddTag/SetTags/RemoveTag emit \\new TPatch() for TrackedResource patches, leaving Tags=null (NRE in the patch fallback path)\",\"body\":\"### Summary\\n\\nThe mgmt code generator emits broken `AddTag` / `SetTags` / `RemoveTag` methods on `ArmResource` types whose backing patch model derives from `TrackedResourceData`. The fallback (non-`TagResource`) branch instantiates the patch via the **internal parameterless constructor** instead of the public `[InitializationConstructor]`, leaving `Tags = null` and causing a `NullReferenceException` at runtime.\\n\\nThis affects **every TrackedResource generated from TypeSpec** when the user-supplied subscription does not have the generic Tags resource available (i.e. `CanUseTagResource(...)` returns `false`).\\n\\n### Repro\\n\\nRepository: `Azure.ResourceManager.ManagedServiceIdentities` (migrated from autorest.csharp to TypeSpec mgmt generator). Run the existing recorded tests in playback:\\n\\n```\\ncd sdk/managedserviceidentity/Azure.ResourceManager.ManagedServiceIdentities/tests\\n$env:AZURE_TEST_MODE = \\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;Playback\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;\\ndotnet test -f net10.0 --filter \\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;FullyQualifiedName~AddTag|FullyQualifiedName~SetTags|FullyQualifiedName~RemoveTag\\u0026#39;\\u0026#39;\\n```\\n\\nResult:\\n```\\nTotal tests: 18\\n     Passed: 12   ← (True) and (null) cases — go through the TagResource branch\\n     Failed:  6   ← (False) cases — go through the patch branch\\n```\\n\\nStack trace for `SetTags(False)`:\\n```\\nSystem.NullReferenceException : Object reference not set to an instance of an object.\\n   at Azure.ResourceManager.SharedExtensions.ReplaceWith(IDictionary`2 dest, IDictionary`2 src)\\n        sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/src/Shared/SharedExtensions.cs:47\\n   at Azure.ResourceManager.ManagedServiceIdentities.UserAssignedIdentityResource.SetTags(IDictionary`2 tags, ...)\\n        sdk/managedserviceidentity/.../src/Generated/UserAssignedIdentityResource.cs:568\\n```\\n\\n### Root cause\\n\\nThe emitted else-branch (in `Generated/UserAssignedIdentityResource.cs`):\\n\\n```csharp\\nelse\\n{\\n    UserAssignedIdentityData current = (await GetAsync(...)).Value.Data;\\n    UserAssignedIdentityPatch patch = new UserAssignedIdentityPatch();   // ← BUG\\n    foreach (KeyValuePair tag in current.Tags)\\n        patch.Tags.Add(tag);                                              // NRE: patch.Tags == null\\n    patch.Tags[key] = value;\\n    Response result = await UpdateAsync(patch, ...);\\n    return Response.FromValue(result.Value, result.GetRawResponse());\\n}\\n```\\n\\n`UserAssignedIdentityPatch` exposes three constructors:\\n\\n| Ctor | Visibility | Purpose | Tags state |\\n|------|------------|---------|------------|\\n| `UserAssignedIdentityPatch(AzureLocation)` | `public` | `[InitializationConstructor]` (chained to `TrackedResourceData(AzureLocation)`) | `Tags = new ChangeTrackingDictionary()` ✅ |\\n| `UserAssignedIdentityPatch(...)` (full args) | `internal` | `[SerializationConstructor]` | uses caller-supplied `tags` |\\n| `UserAssignedIdentityPatch()` | `internal` | parameterless deserialization ctor (chains to `TrackedResourceData()`) | `Tags = null` ❌ |\\n\\n`TrackedResourceData` (in `Azure.ResourceManager`):\\n\\n```csharp\\n[InitializationConstructor]\\nprotected TrackedResourceData(AzureLocation location)\\n{\\n    Tags = new ChangeTrackingDictionary();\\n    Location = location;\\n}\\n\\ninternal protected TrackedResourceData()\\n{\\n    // intentionally empty — for deserialization only\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe generator already understands `[InitializationConstructor]` vs the deserialization ctor (it emits both). But its tag-method template ignores that distinction and always picks the parameterless ctor — which is `internal` and intended only for the deserialization framework to call before populating properties.\\n\\n### Fix\\n\\nIn the emitter template that produces `AddTag` / `SetTags` / `RemoveTag` (sync + async × 3), when the patch type derives from `TrackedResourceData`, emit:\\n\\n```diff\\n- UserAssignedIdentityPatch patch = new UserAssignedIdentityPatch();\\n+ UserAssignedIdentityPatch patch = new UserAssignedIdentityPatch(current.Location);\\n```\\n\\nThat is, instantiate the patch via its `[InitializationConstructor]` and pass `current.Data.Location` (already fetched on the line above). For non-tracked patches the existing `new TPatch()` remains correct.\\n\\n### Affected scope\\n\\nEvery mgmt SDK migrated to the TypeSpec mgmt generator whose resource is a `TrackedResource` and whose service does not expose a tags-only PATCH operation (so the generator falls back to building the patch object client-side). It compiles and ApiCompat passes, so the bug is invisible unless the package has tag-method tests that force the `else` branch.\\n\\n### Workaround in the meantime\\n\\nIn `src/Custom/{Resource}.cs`, `[CodeGenSuppress]` the 6 tag methods and re-implement them with `new TPatch(current.Location)`. See `sdk/managedserviceidentity/Azure.ResourceManager.ManagedServiceIdentities/src/Custom/UserAssignedIdentityResource.cs` for an example.\\n\\n### Environment\\n- Generator: `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt@1.0.0-alpha.20260421.1`\\n- TypeSpec compiler resolved at: `sdk/managedserviceidentity/.../TempTypeSpecFiles/node_modules/@typespec/compiler`\\n- Repro repo: `Azure/azure-sdk-for-net`, branch migrating `Azure.ResourceManager.ManagedServiceIdentities` from autorest.csharp to TypeSpec\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58681\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"haiyuazhang\",\"id\":1924967,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/haiyuazhang\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1924967?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\",\"CodeGen\"],\"assignees\":[\"haiyuazhang\",\"Copilot\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":2,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":2},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-25T18:17:33Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-25T18:19:13Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
